_methodology: Each metric is a 0–100 composite score synthesized by Claude from multiple publicly available data sources, structured datasets, expert knowledge, and qualitative assessment. Sub-indicators are weighted within each metric as specified below. Countries with insufficient data receive null. Scores are designed to be reproducible: re-running with the same methodology and data sources should yield comparable results within ±5 points.

metrics.name: Nomad & Expat Community
metrics.description: Measures the density and vibrancy of digital nomad and expatriate communities. Countries with established coworking ecosystems, nomad hubs, coliving spaces, and active expat networks score higher.

subIndicators.name: coworkingDensity
subIndicators.description: Density of coworking spaces per capita and per major city, sourced from Coworker.com, Coworkly, and NomadList listings

subIndicators.name: nomadHubPresence
subIndicators.description: Presence of recognized nomad hub cities (from NomadList top-100, Reddit r/digitalnomad consensus, and Lonely Planet digital nomad guides)

subIndicators.name: colivingAvailability
subIndicators.description: Availability of coliving spaces (Coliving.com, NomadList coliving listings, Outsite, Selina network)

subIndicators.name: expatCommunitySize
subIndicators.description: Estimated expat population relative to total population, international school density, InterNations community activity, and Facebook expat group sizes

metrics.normalization: Linear 0–100 composite of weighted sub-indicators. Each sub-indicator is independently scored 0–100 then combined.
dataSources: NomadList (nomadlist.com) — city rankings and coworking/coliving data

metrics.name: Visa & Stay Flexibility
metrics.description: Assesses how easy it is for a digital nomad (holding a strong passport like US/EU) to enter, stay long-term, and extend their stay. Considers tourist visa duration, visa-run feasibility, e-visa availability, dedicated nomad visa programs, and paths to longer residency.

subIndicators.name: touristVisaLength
subIndicators.description: Standard tourist visa duration in days for US/EU passport holders (Henley, Sherpa, IATA data)

subIndicators.name: visaRunEase
subIndicators.description: Feasibility and legality of visa runs — proximity to border, tolerance by immigration authorities, land-border accessibility

subIndicators.name: eVisaAvailability
subIndicators.description: Whether e-visa or visa-on-arrival is available for major passport holders (Henley Passport Index, VisaGuide.World)

subIndicators.name: nomadVisaProgram
subIndicators.description: Existence and quality of a dedicated digital nomad visa — duration, cost, income threshold, renewal options

subIndicators.name: pathToResidency
subIndicators.description: Availability of investor/freelancer/entrepreneur residency routes beyond tourist stays

metrics.normalization: Linear 0–100 composite. nomadVisaProgram scores 0 if no program exists, 50–100 based on program quality if it does.
dataSources: IATA Timatic visa database (via airline tools)
Existing nomadVisa.json and visas.json in project data

metrics.name: Nomad Cost-Value Ratio
metrics.description: Not just cheapness — this measures the quality of nomad life per dollar spent. Countries where you get excellent coworking, reliable fast internet, good food, and comfortable housing at a reasonable price score highest. An expensive country with world-class nomad infrastructure can still score well.

subIndicators.name: monthlyNomadBudget
subIndicators.description: Estimated monthly cost for a comfortable nomad lifestyle (private room, coworking, meals, transport) — NomadList cost data, Numbeo, Expatistan

subIndicators.name: qualityPerDollar
subIndicators.description: Ratio of infrastructure quality (internet speed, coworking quality, housing standard) to monthly cost

subIndicators.name: internetValueRatio
subIndicators.description: Average internet speed (Speedtest Global Index) relative to the cost of a reliable connection

subIndicators.name: foodAndAccommodationValue
subIndicators.description: Quality and variety of food/accommodation options relative to price — restaurant diversity, Airbnb median prices, local food quality indices

metrics.normalization: Composite 0–100. High quality at low cost → top scores. High quality at high cost → moderate. Low quality at low cost → moderate. Low quality at high cost → low.

metrics.name: Remote Work & Lifestyle
metrics.description: Evaluates how well a country supports the remote work lifestyle beyond just internet. Considers timezone overlap with major client regions (US/EU), café work culture, outdoor recreation access, social scene for nomads, and overall work-life rhythm.

subIndicators.name: timezoneOverlap
subIndicators.description: Hours of business overlap with US East (ET) and Central European (CET) working hours. UTC-1 to UTC+3 score highest for EU overlap; UTC-4 to UTC-8 for US overlap. Best = overlap with both.

subIndicators.name: cafeCulture
subIndicators.description: Prevalence of laptop-friendly cafés with wifi, power outlets, and reasonable stay expectations — qualitative assessment from nomad community consensus

subIndicators.name: outdoorRecreation
subIndicators.description: Access to beaches, mountains, hiking, surfing, diving, and outdoor sports — geographic and climate-based assessment

subIndicators.name: socialScene
subIndicators.description: Nightlife, meetup culture, dating scene accessibility, and general social vibrancy for foreigners

subIndicators.name: weekendEscapes
subIndicators.description: Proximity to diverse weekend trip destinations — beaches, nature, cultural sites, neighboring countries reachable in 1-3 hours

dataSources: Digital nomad community surveys and blog consensus

metrics.name: Digital Nomad Infrastructure
metrics.description: Goes beyond basic internet penetration (already covered by 'infrastructure'). Measures the digital ecosystem specifically relevant to nomads: café wifi reliability, mobile data quality and eSIM support, digital payment adoption, app ecosystem for daily life, and tech-forward city infrastructure.

subIndicators.name: cafeWifiReliability
subIndicators.description: Average wifi speed and reliability in cafés and public spaces — Speedtest data, NomadList wifi scores, nomad community reports

subIndicators.name: mobileDataQuality
subIndicators.description: 4G/5G coverage, average mobile speeds, and prepaid SIM/eSIM availability and cost for tourists (Opensignal, GSMA data)

subIndicators.name: digitalPayments
subIndicators.description: Adoption of contactless payments, mobile wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay), and card acceptance rate vs cash-only economy

subIndicators.name: eSIMSupport
subIndicators.description: eSIM availability from local carriers, Airalo/Holafly coverage, ease of getting connected on arrival

subIndicators.name: appEcosystem
subIndicators.description: Availability of essential apps: ride-hailing (Uber/Bolt/local), food delivery, maps, translation, banking — practical digital daily life

dataSources: Speedtest Global Index (Ookla) — fixed and mobile

metrics.name: Cultural Openness to Foreigners
metrics.description: Measures how welcoming and navigable daily life is for a foreign remote worker. Not about tourist attractions — about real daily-life friction: can you get things done without speaking the local language? Are locals friendly to foreigners in daily interactions? Is there bureaucratic hassle? Are scams/tourist-targeting common?

subIndicators.name: dailyLifeFriendliness
subIndicators.description: General friendliness and helpfulness toward foreigners in daily interactions — InterNations Expat Insider friendliness rankings, nomad community consensus

subIndicators.name: languageAccessibility
subIndicators.description: How easy it is to navigate daily life (not tourism — groceries, banking, healthcare, bureaucracy) without the local language. English signage, bilingual service staff prevalence

subIndicators.name: scamPrevalence
subIndicators.description: Prevalence of tourist/foreigner-targeting scams, taxi overcharging, dual pricing — qualitative assessment from nomad/expat community reports and travel advisories

subIndicators.name: bureaucraticEase
subIndicators.description: Ease of dealing with local bureaucracy as a foreigner — opening a bank account, getting a SIM card, registering an address, dealing with police. World Bank Doing Business (legacy), expat surveys

metrics.normalization: Linear 0–100 composite. Lower scam prevalence and bureaucratic friction → higher scores.
dataSources: Transparency International CPI (corruption perception as proxy for bureaucratic friction)
Digital nomad forum consensus (Reddit, Facebook groups)
US State Department / UK FCO travel advisories
